response.justification: Bogdan presents a strong match on the AI-native and distributed-systems dimensions of the role: his current position at re:cinq directly involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP-based agentic tooling, and his time at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic demonstrates real high-throughput distributed systems work at millions of HTTP requests per second. However, Python is listed as a must-have technical skill and is entirely absent from his resume, skills inventory, and project history — his primary languages are TypeScript/JavaScript and DLang. That gap is material for a must-have requirement. If Python proficiency can be confirmed or is genuinely close at hand, he warrants a full interview loop; otherwise the technical mismatch is a blocking concern.
strengths: Current role at re:cinq directly involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators for agentic workflows — meets the AI-native engineering baseline the role treats as a given, not a differentiator
Proven high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O, caching, and low-latency design
Consistent Staff-level titles and broad platform coverage across web, mobile, embedded, and cloud demonstrate the architectural breadth and systems thinking the role requires
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the resume, skills list, and all project descriptions — a stated must-have skill with no evidence of even working-level exposure
Stakeholder management across functions, cross-functional coordination with product and leadership, and operational ownership including on-call are not evidenced anywhere in the resume

Current Staff role at re:cinq started April 2025, leaving the scope of technical-strategy ownership at scale unverified; prior Staff titles at Altom and re:cinq skew toward hands-on IC work rather than domain-level strategy
